all_ports.description: Specifies whether to forward all ports. Must be set to true or false.

allow_global_access.description: Specifies whether the forwarding rule allows access from clients worldwide. Set to true for global access or false for regional access.

allow_psc_global_access.description: Controls whether global access is enabled for Private Service Connect connections; must be true or false.

backend_service.description: Specifies the backend service to which the forwarding rule directs traffic. Must be the name or self_link of an existing backend service in the same region.

description.description: Specifies the forwarding rule's purpose or function, detailing its role in directing traffic to a target resource.

ip_address.description: Specifies the static IP address to associate with the forwarding rule. Must be an existing regional or global IP address resource.

ip_collection.description: List of IP addresses or IP ranges to be used by the forwarding rule; must be valid and conform to the required format.

ip_protocol.description: Specifies the IP protocol for the forwarding rule; must be one of TCP, UDP, ESP, AH, SCTP, ICMP, or L3_DEFAULT.

ip_version.description: Specifies the IP version for the forwarding rule; must be either IPV4 or IPV6.

is_mirroring_collector.description: Indicates if the forwarding rule is used as a mirroring collector; must be set to true for packet mirroring configurations.

labels.description: Key-value pairs for organizing and categorizing resources, such as by environment or project; must be strings.

load_balancing_scheme.description: Defines the load balancing configuration, specifying whether the forwarding rule is internal or external.

name.description: Specifies the name of the forwarding rule, which must be unique within the project and region.

network.description: Specifies the network to which the forwarding rule is attached. Must be a valid network resource in the same project and region.

network_tier.description: Specifies the network tier for the forwarding rule, such as PREMIUM or STANDARD.

no_automate_dns_zone.description: Indicates whether DNS records are automatically managed for the forwarding rule; set to false to manage DNS manually.

port_range.description: Defines the range of ports for the forwarding rule, specified as a single port or a range (e.g., "80" or "3000-4000"). Must be valid TCP/UDP ports.

ports.description: List of ports for forwarding traffic; must be specified as a range or individual values.

recreate_closed_psc.description: Controls whether the forwarding rule should be recreated when its target service is closed. Set to true to force recreation in such scenarios.

service_directory_registrations.description: Controls whether Service Directory registrations are enabled for the forwarding rule; requires Service Directory API to be enabled.

service_label.description: Specifies a label to identify the forwarding rule's service, used for organizing and managing resources. Must be unique within the region and comply with label naming conventions.

source_ip_ranges.description: Defines the CIDR IP ranges that are allowed to access the forwarding rule, ensuring traffic matches specified source addresses.

subnetwork.description: Specifies the subnetwork to associate with the forwarding rule; must exist in the same region as the forwarding rule.

target.description: Specifies the target resource to which traffic is directed; must be a valid target proxy or target pool.

timeouts.description: Defines the time duration for creating, updating, or deleting the forwarding rule; supports custom timeout settings to ensure operations complete within the specified limits.
